# Epora Wallet

> Epora Wallet is a non-custodial, permission-based wallet on Cardano. It keeps funds in an on-chain smart contract and lets one wallet be shared across people with different roles: owners control the rules, spenders can spend up to a daily limit, and backup people can recover access if owners lose their keys. The current Preprod prototype supports core smart-wallet flows including wallet creation, funding, spending, multi-signature approvals, scheduled payments, and a "wake-up timer" dead-man switch for inheritance and recovery. Staking, governance, public API/spec, and WalletConnect signing are still active-development areas. You authorize transactions with your own Cardano wallet; Epora never takes custody of your keys or funds.

The project is built on Cardano and is currently deployed on the Preprod test network. The smart contracts and frontend are open source, developed under a Project Catalyst grant.

## What it does

- **Shared control**: One wallet owned and used by multiple people with distinct roles.
- **Owners**: Manage the wallet and can change every rule.
- **Spenders**: Spend up to a daily limit set by an owner.
- **Backup people**: Recover access if owners lose their keys, after a wake-up timer expires.
- **Daily spending limits**: Per-spender caps prevent runaway transfers.
- **Scheduled payments**: Send a fixed amount on a recurring schedule (rent, subscriptions, payroll).
- **Multi-signature approvals**: Require more than one signer before sensitive actions go through.
- **Wake-up timer (dead-man switch)**: If no owner signs for a configurable period, backup people can step in to recover the wallet.
- **Staking and governance**: Experimental surfaces are present, but end-to-end staking/governance flows are not yet public-release complete.

## Who it is for

- Families and partners who want a shared wallet with role-based limits.
- Teams and DAOs that need spending limits without losing recovery options.
- Individuals who want a dead-man switch on their Cardano funds so heirs or backups can recover.
- Anyone building on Cardano who needs permission rules without writing custom Plutus contracts.

## Status

- Stage: Active development.
- Network: Cardano Preprod test network. Not yet on Cardano mainnet.
- License: MIT. See [LICENSE](https://github.com/schaier-io/cardano-permission-based-wallet/blob/main/LICENSE).
- Funded by: Project Catalyst Fund 11 — "Dead-man-switch Permission Based Wallet".

## Key routes

- [Wallet home](/user) — Main wallet interface.
- [Proposal workspace](/user/proposals) — Multi-signature proposal review and signing.

## Resources

- [Catalyst proposal](https://projectcatalyst.io/funds/11/cardano-use-cases-concept/dead-man-switch-permission-based-wallet)
- [Discord](https://discord.gg/2uh4BynQBW)
- [X](https://x.com/eporawallet)

## Glossary

- **Owner** — A person with full control of the wallet's rules and funds.
- **Spender** — A person who can spend up to a daily limit but cannot change wallet rules.
- **Backup person** — A person who can recover the wallet after the wake-up timer expires.
- **Fund pool** — A group of funds inside the wallet, used to organize money by purpose.
- **Wake-up timer** — The inactivity period after which backup people can step in. Also called a dead-man switch.
- **Receipt code** — A unique code identifying a wallet's first transaction, used to find or import the wallet.
- **Preprod** — Cardano's pre-production test network. Funds on preprod have no monetary value.
